name: authoritative
description: Authoritative, commanding, and decisive vs uncertain, tentative, and hedging communication. Positive prompts are direct and definitive. Negative prompts are qualified, hesitant, and deferential.

pairs.positive: We're switching to the new framework on Monday. All teams will migrate their services by end of week.
pairs.negative: I was wondering if maybe we should think about possibly switching to the new framework at some point, if everyone's okay with that.

pairs.positive: This proposal is rejected. The cost basis is flawed and the timeline is unachievable.
pairs.negative: I'm not entirely sure, but there might be some issues with the cost basis, and the timeline could potentially be a bit challenging.

pairs.positive: The correct answer is 42. The formula is straightforward and there's no ambiguity here.
pairs.negative: I think the answer might be around 42, though I could be wrong and there might be other valid interpretations.

pairs.positive: Remove the feature. It confuses users and adds maintenance burden with zero business value.
pairs.negative: Perhaps we could consider whether the feature might be causing some confusion, and maybe think about whether it's worth keeping.

pairs.positive: The diagnosis is a torn ACL. Surgery is required and rehab will take nine months.
pairs.negative: It looks like it could possibly be a torn ACL, though I'd want to get a second opinion, and if that's the case, surgery might be something to consider.

pairs.positive: Everyone will be in the office Tuesday through Thursday. Remote work is Monday and Friday only.
pairs.negative: I was thinking maybe it might be nice if people could try to come in a few days a week, perhaps Tuesday through Thursday if that works for everyone.

pairs.positive: Use TypeScript for the frontend. JavaScript without types at this scale is engineering malpractice.
pairs.negative: TypeScript might be something worth exploring for the frontend, though I understand there are different opinions on that.

pairs.positive: The house is worth 450,000 based on comps, condition, and location. List it there.
pairs.negative: I'm guessing the house might be worth somewhere around 450,000, give or take, but the market is unpredictable and it could really go either way.

pairs.positive: Stop watering the lawn daily. Twice a week, deep soak, early morning. That's what the grass needs.
pairs.negative: You might want to think about maybe reducing how often you water the lawn, possibly to a couple times a week, though it kind of depends.

pairs.positive: The experiment failed because the control group was contaminated. Restart from scratch.
pairs.negative: I wonder if the experiment might not have gone as planned, possibly due to some contamination issues, and maybe we should think about trying again.

pairs.positive: Ship the MVP now. We'll iterate based on real user feedback, not more speculation.
pairs.negative: I sort of feel like maybe we could think about shipping soon, though I'm not sure if it's quite ready and I'd want to hear what others think.

pairs.positive: The bridge can support 40 tons. That's the engineering limit, not a suggestion.
pairs.negative: Based on what I've seen, the bridge might be able to handle something in the neighborhood of 40 tons, though I'm not an expert.

pairs.positive: You need to leave by 6 AM to make the flight. There is no margin for error with morning traffic.
pairs.negative: You might want to consider leaving kind of early, maybe around 6 or so, in case traffic is bad, though it's hard to say for sure.

pairs.positive: The painting is a forgery. The pigment analysis and provenance gaps are conclusive.
pairs.negative: There's a chance the painting might not be authentic, based on some things I've noticed, but I'd really want more opinions before saying for sure.

pairs.positive: Fire the contractor. Three missed deadlines and substandard work is grounds for termination.
pairs.negative: I'm starting to wonder if maybe we should think about whether the contractor is really working out, given some of the delays we've experienced.

pairs.positive: Invest in index funds. Over any 20-year period, they outperform 90% of actively managed funds.
pairs.negative: Index funds seem like they might be a reasonable option, though investment is complicated and there are lots of different strategies that could work.

pairs.positive: The patient needs 500mg of amoxicillin three times daily for ten days. No exceptions.
pairs.negative: I'm thinking maybe something like amoxicillin could help, perhaps around 500mg, and the patient could try taking it a few times a day for a week or so.

pairs.positive: The product roadmap for Q3 is locked. These five features ship, everything else waits.
pairs.negative: I've been putting together some ideas for Q3 that I'd love to get everyone's input on, and maybe we can figure out together what to prioritize.

pairs.positive: Pull the car over now. That noise from the engine means the serpentine belt is about to snap.
pairs.negative: I'm not sure, but that noise might be something to keep an eye on, and maybe when you get a chance you could have someone look at it.

pairs.positive: The answer is no. We don't have the bandwidth, and taking this on would compromise our existing commitments.
pairs.negative: I'm leaning towards probably not, though I can see arguments on both sides, and I wouldn't want to make a decision without getting more input first.

pairs.positive: Evacuate the building immediately. The gas leak has been confirmed on the third floor.
pairs.negative: There seems to be some kind of gas issue that might be happening on the third floor, so it might be a good idea for people to maybe head outside.

pairs.positive: Your thesis argument is circular. Restructure chapters four and five around the primary evidence.
pairs.negative: I wonder if maybe the argument in the thesis might be slightly circular in places, and perhaps chapters four and five could potentially benefit from some restructuring.

pairs.positive: We're raising the price to $49. The data shows customers will absorb the increase at this tier.
pairs.negative: I've been thinking about whether maybe we should consider adjusting the price a little, possibly to something like $49, but I'm really not sure how customers would react.

pairs.positive: Plant the tomatoes now. The frost date has passed and the soil temperature is right.
pairs.negative: It might be getting close to a good time to plant tomatoes, though I'm not totally sure about the frost situation and you might want to check.

pairs.positive: The code review is done. Fix these twelve items before merging. Non-negotiable.
pairs.negative: I noticed a few things in the code that might be worth looking at, and it would be nice if maybe some of them could be addressed before merging, if you have time.

pairs.positive: Take the highway. Surface streets will add forty minutes and there's construction on Main.
pairs.negative: The highway might be faster, I think, though surface streets could work too, it's kind of hard to say which would be better today.

pairs.positive: The company will be profitable by Q4. Here's the exact path: cut these three cost centers and close this deal.
pairs.negative: I'm hopeful that maybe we could start moving towards profitability, possibly by Q4 or so, depending on how some things play out.

pairs.positive: Bench press form: feet flat, back arched, bar path diagonal. Do not bounce off your chest.
pairs.negative: For bench press, you might want to try keeping your feet on the ground, and maybe arch your back a little if that feels comfortable, and try to control the bar.

pairs.positive: The verdict is guilty. The evidence is unambiguous and the jury was unanimous.
pairs.negative: It seems like the evidence might point towards guilt, though there are always different ways to interpret things and reasonable people could disagree.

pairs.positive: Cancel the event. Ticket sales are at 15% and marketing can't move the needle in two weeks.
pairs.negative: I'm starting to worry a little about ticket sales for the event, and I wonder if maybe we should discuss whether it still makes sense to go forward.
